generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuates vehicle components including motor, steering servo, and braking systems based on real-time control signals from the Digital Twin Entity"
]

instance SatelliteThrusters : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides orbital maneuvering capabilities for collision avoidance and debris tracking in aerospace applications"
]

instance BatteryManagementSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages charging/discharging cycles and thermal regulation for electric vehicle battery systems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryDegradationModel
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LidarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects 3D point cloud data for vehicle environment perception and object tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LidarPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors motor actuation, steering servo angles, and vehicle state parameters"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance ThermalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures battery and component temperatures for thermal management in automotive systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ThermalData
]

instance OrbitalDebrisSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Detects and tracks space debris using radar and optical sensors for aerospace applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DebrisTrackingData
]

instance IoTSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects manufacturing process parameters from IoT devices in ISO 23247-compliant factories"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ProcessParameterData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LidarPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LidarSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ThermalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ThermalSensor
]

instance DebrisTrackingData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OrbitalDebrisSensor
]

instance ProcessParameterData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IoTSensorNetwork
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DigitalTwinEntityModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Core digital representation of Observable Manufacturing Elements following ISO 23247 Part 3, integrating geometric, behavioral, and analytical models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation models including finite element analysis (FEA) and computational fluid dynamics (CFD) for automotive/aerospace applications"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinSimulationEngine
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning-enhanced prognostic health management (PHM) models for failure prediction in automotive components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MLModelTrainingEnabler
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time trajectory optimization models for aerospace collision avoidance and debris tracking"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AerospaceControlEnabler
]

instance BatteryDegradationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Performance forecasting model for electric vehicle battery degradation tracking"
]

instance RealTimeTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor data streams from physical systems including vehicle health diagnostics and manufacturing process parameters"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarPointCloudData, VehicleStateData, ThermalData, ProcessParameterData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from raw LiDAR point clouds for visualization and collision avoidance"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationServiceEnabler
]

instance DebrisOrbitData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed orbital data of space debris for collision avoidance calculations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DebrisTrackingData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AerospaceControlEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingProcessData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time manufacturing process data for optimization and quality control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessParameterData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessOptimizationServiceEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant entity responsible for interfacing with physical sensors and actuators, implementing OPC UA protocols for data exchange"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeTelemetryService
]

instance DigitalTwinSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine supporting physics-based and AI-enhanced simulations using ANSYS, Simcenter, and TensorFlow frameworks"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsSimulationService, AIEnhancedSimulationService
]

instance MLModelTrainingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Federated learning framework enabling distributed AI model training across digital twins using TensorFlow Federated"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance AerospaceControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge computing-based control system for aerospace applications using NVIDIA EGX and NASA STK for trajectory calculations"
    DTDFVocab:enables CollisionAvoidanceService, OrbitalDebrisTrackingService
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data preprocessing pipeline implementing normalization, filtering, and clustering for raw sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D/4D visualization engine using Unity and Unreal Engine for real-time digital replica rendering"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance ProcessOptimizationServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization service enabler for manufacturing process optimization"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessOptimizationService
]

instance EdgeComputingNode : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant edge node for low-latency processing using Kubernetes-based edge clusters"
    DTDFVocab:enables EdgeBasedMonitoringService
]

instance StandardizedAPIGateway : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-aligned API gateway ensuring interoperability between digital twin components and external systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables ServiceIntegrationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeTelemetryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time telemetry monitoring for vehicle health diagnostics and battery state-of-charge tracking"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleHealthInsight, BatterySoCInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers predictive maintenance alerts using machine learning-enhanced failure prediction models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentFailureInsight
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitors environmental conditions including thermal management and orbital debris tracking"
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalConditionInsight, DebrisCollisionWarning
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimizes manufacturing processes for energy efficiency using digital twin simulations"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessEfficiencyInsight
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimizes spacecraft trajectories for debris avoidance using real-time digital twin models"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance ResourceAllocationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Manages battery charging schedules and resource allocation for electric vehicles"
    DTDFVocab:provides ChargingScheduleInsight
]

instance RealTimeAdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time adaptive control for autonomous braking systems and spacecraft maneuvering"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterInsight
]

instance CollisionAvoidanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements collision avoidance algorithms for both automotive and aerospace applications"
    DTDFVocab:provides CollisionRiskInsight
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers 3D/4D digital replicas and interactive dashboards for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationDataInsight
]

instance PhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes physics-based simulations including finite element analysis and computational fluid dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultsInsight
]

instance AIEnhancedSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performs AI-enhanced simulations using neural network-driven predictive models"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationEnhancementInsight
]

instance DigitalTwinAsAService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides digital twin-as-a-service for third-party validation and testing of automotive components"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationResultsInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time monitoring of system health and performance metrics"
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers visualization capabilities for digital twin data"
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides control functionality for system operations"
]

instance QualityControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures quality control through monitoring and analysis of manufacturing processes"
]

instance DeviceControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Controls physical devices and actuators based on digital twin commands"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects anomalies in system data and operations"
]

instance OrbitalDebrisTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Tracks space debris and provides collision avoidance data"
]

instance TrainingSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides simulation environments for training and testing"
]

instance RealTimeControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time control capabilities for autonomous systems"
]

instance EdgeBasedMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitors system operations at the edge computing layer"
]

instance ServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrates services across different domains and systems"
]

    base:desc "Facilitates communication between different domains and systems"
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

    base:desc "Real-time health status of vehicle components including engine, transmission, and suspension systems"
]

    base:desc "Provides optimization recommendations for manufacturing processes"

// C12: Environments/Components
instance AutomotiveEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehiclePhysicalTwin, AutomotiveDigitalTwin, AutomotiveUserInterface
]

instance AerospaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SatellitePhysicalTwin, AerospaceDigitalTwin, MissionControlInterface
]

instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains FactoryPhysicalTwin, ManufacturingDigitalTwin, ProductionUserInterface
]

instance VehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical representation of automotive vehicles including sensors, actuators, and embedded systems"
    base:isContainedIn AutomotiveEnvironment
]

instance AutomotiveDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of automotive systems following ISO 23247 standards"
    base:isContainedIn AutomotiveEnvironment
]

instance AutomotiveUserInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User-facing components including 3D visualization and control panels for vehicle operators"
    base:isContainedIn AutomotiveEnvironment
]

instance SatellitePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical representation of satellites including sensors, thrusters, and communication systems"
    base:isContainedIn AerospaceEnvironment
]

instance AerospaceDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of aerospace systems for collision avoidance and debris tracking"
    base:isContainedIn AerospaceEnvironment
]

instance MissionControlInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface components for aerospace mission control including visualization and control dashboards"
    base:isContainedIn AerospaceEnvironment
]

instance FactoryPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical representation of manufacturing facilities including equipment and production lines"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance ManufacturingDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of manufacturing processes following ISO 23247 standards"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance ProductionUserInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface components for production engineers including monitoring and optimization tools"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is facilitated by the **Controlling FE** (ACC - Adaptive Cruise Control), which translates digital commands into physical actuation. Key aspects include:

- **Command Types**:
  Control signals for **motor actuation** and **steering servo actuation**, derived from processed digital twin data (e.g., object tracks, projected driving paths).

- **Control Message Formats**:
  Commands are serialized and aligned via the **Data Translation** component (ISO 23247), ensuring compatibility with physical actuators.
  No explicit mention of specific message formats (e.g., CAN, MODBUS), but alignment implies standardized ROS2/ROS1-compatible messages.

- **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
  Not explicitly described in the document. Assumed to rely on the **Data Translation** component’s serialization/alignment for consistency, but no cryptographic or access control mechanisms are mentioned.

- **Feedback Systems**:
  The system lacks explicit feedback loops from physical actuators to the digital twin. However, **vehicle state data** (e.g., motor/servo positions) is continuously transmitted back to the digital twin for real-time monitoring and recalibration.

- **Conflict Resolution**:
  Not addressed in the document. Potential conflicts (e.g., conflicting control commands) are not mitigated beyond the **Data Translation** component’s alignment processes.

- **Command Execution Confirmation**:
  Not explicitly described. Assumed to rely on **vehicle state data** as indirect confirmation of command execution (e.g., motor/servo positions matching commanded values).
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination. This temporal constraint is mandated due to hardware limitations and aligns with stricter vehicular application standards. The system’s temporal granularity is dictated by the ROS2-based implementation, where each Function Entity (FE) executes as an independent ROS2 package with nodes handling real-time data processing (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the ROS2 communication backbone, which relies on inter-node message passing with no explicit latency guarantees mentioned beyond the 100 ms synchronization constraint. Time scales for services are not explicitly detailed beyond the synchronization requirement, but the architecture assumes near-real-time execution for safety-critical vehicular operations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)-inspired, entity-based orchestration pattern**, where Function Entities (FEs) are mapped to ROS2 packages and executed as independent nodes. The architecture emphasizes **modularity and hardware-specific deployment**, with each entity (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User) deployed on distinct hardware. Key coordination mechanisms include:
  - **ROS2-based inter-node communication** for intra-system messaging between FEs.
  - **Entity-specific resource ownership**: The *Physical Twin Entity* hosts general automotive functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*), diverging from ISO 23247’s OME (Observation and Measurement Entity) to encompass broader system resources.
  - **Deviations from ISO 23247**: The *Data Storage* component lacks a direct FE alignment, and the *User Interface FE* is split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* subcomponents. Resource management is implicit, tied to hardware deployment constraints rather than explicit orchestration frameworks. The system lacks mention of dynamic reconfiguration, federated learning, or cross-twin coordination protocols."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of the Digital Twin (DT) system is structured according to the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing** standard, specifically Part 2 (Reference Architecture). The lifecycle is entity-based, where each **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**—such as equipment, facilities, products, or processes—undergoes representation through a modular architecture composed of four primary entities: **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, **User Entity**, and **Functional Entities (FEs)**. The lifecycle stages are implicitly defined by the **development, operation, and evolution** of these entities:

1. **Development Phase**:
   - **Design and Modeling**: The OME is digitally represented via the **Digital Twin Entity**, which implements a virtual model aligned with the physical counterpart’s observable attributes (e.g., operational states, performance metrics).
   - **Entity Composition**: The system decomposes functionality into **Functional Entities (FEs)**, which may include tasks like data transformation (e.g., converting raw sensor data into human-interpretable formats), OME actuation, or user interface mediation. The standard does not prescribe implementation specifics but suggests protocols like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for interoperability.
   - **Integration**: The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** interfaces with physical sensors/actuators to bridge the gap between the OME and its digital twin, while the **User Entity** provides interfaces for monitoring and control.

2. **Operation Phase**:
   - **Real-Time Synchronization**: The Digital Twin Entity continuously updates its model using data streams from the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, ensuring temporal alignment with the OME’s physical state.
   - **User Interaction**: The **User Entity** enables operators to query the Digital Twin Entity for insights (e.g., predictive maintenance alerts) or issue commands (e.g., adjusting process parameters via the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**).
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Transition management between phases is implied through the modular entity architecture, allowing incremental updates to individual FEs or entities without disrupting the entire system. The standard does not explicitly outline governance frameworks but emphasizes **entity-based scalability** and **modularity** as key principles.

3. **Evolution Phase**:
   - **Version Control**: While not explicitly detailed, the entity-based design facilitates versioning of FEs or Digital Twin Entities, enabling backward compatibility and phased upgrades. For example, a new FE for advanced analytics could be integrated without altering core entities.
   - **Adaptive Representation**: The Digital Twin Entity’s model may evolve to incorporate higher-fidelity simulations (e.g., physics-based models) or additional OME attributes as requirements change, driven by updates to the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** or **User Entity** interfaces.

The lifecycle governance approach is implicitly **entity-centric**, leveraging modularity to manage transitions between phases while maintaining interoperability through standardized interfaces (e.g., OPC UA). Transition management relies on the **separation of concerns** between entities, ensuring that changes to one component (e.g., a FE for data transformation) do not cascade unpredictably across the system.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **ISO 23247 standard** implicitly addresses **fidelity and validity** through its **entity-based reference architecture**, which mandates that **Digital Twin Entities (DTEs)** must accurately reflect **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**. While the standard does not specify **validation and verification (V&V) methods**, it implies that **model fidelity** is ensured via **real-time data synchronization** between the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** and the DTE. **Uncertainty sources** (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations) are not explicitly quantified, but the architecture suggests **error detection mechanisms** could be embedded in **Functional Entities (FEs)** responsible for data transformation or control. The standard does not detail **quality assurance processes**, but the **User Entity’s role** in providing **human-interpretable interfaces** implies a need for **data consistency checks** to maintain trust in the Digital Twin’s representations. No explicit mention of **uncertainty quantification tools** (e.g., Bayesian methods, confidence intervals) or **error correction protocols** (e.g., Kalman filters, machine learning-based anomaly detection) is provided."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure is defined by the following specifications:

- **Communication Protocols**:
  - **Inter-entity (Physical/Digital)**: Wi-Fi with **UDP transport layer** for raw sensor data and control signals.
  - **Intra-system (SW Components)**: **ROS2** for message-passing between software components (e.g., Data Translation, Clustering & Tracking).

- **Network Topology and Architecture**:
  A **multi-tier architecture** with:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators) communicating via Wi-Fi to the digital twin.
  2. **Digital Twin Entity** (SW components) running on a ROS2 network, where **Data Translation** acts as the central hub for serialization/alignment.
  3. **User Entity** (visualization/control interfaces) interacting via the same ROS2 network.

- **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
  Not explicitly quantified, but real-time operation implies:
  - **Low-latency** (<100ms, inferred from real-time visualization updates).
  - **Moderate bandwidth** (sufficient for LiDAR point clouds and vehicle state data streams).

- **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
  Not described. Assumed to rely on UDP’s best-effort delivery for inter-entity communication, with no explicit redundancy (e.g., failover, acknowledgments).

- **Security Protocols**:
  Not mentioned. No encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms are referenced for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is characterized by the following:

- **Hosting Platforms**:
  Not explicitly stated. Implied to be **on-premise** (given the mention of a **Physical Twin Entity** and localized sensor/actuator integration) with potential **edge deployment** for ROS2-based SW components due to real-time requirements.

- **Computational Requirements**:
  - **Data Processing**: Dedicated SW components for **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, **Data Translation**, and **Controlling FE** (ACC), requiring moderate-to-high CPU/GPU resources for real-time LiDAR processing and control logic.
  - **Visualization**: **3D Visualization** and **User Interface FE** demand GPU acceleration for rendering object tracks and driving paths.

- **Storage Requirements**:
  - **Data Storage**: Implicitly required for **Report Generation** and **Data Collection** (e.g., storing point cloud snapshots, vehicle state logs, and reports).
  - **No explicit capacity mentioned**, but inferred to scale with sensor data volume (e.g., LiDAR point clouds).

- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  Not described. The system appears monolithic with no mention of microservices, containerization (e.g., Docker), or cloud scalability (e.g., Kubernetes). Scalability is likely limited to hardware upgrades.

- **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **Single-node deployment** (ROS2-based components co-located on a single machine).
  - **Distributed deployment** (Physical Twin Entity communicates via Wi-Fi to a central digital twin node).

- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  Not addressed. No mention of fault tolerance, backup systems, or high-availability clustering. Reliability depends on hardware uptime and UDP’s best-effort delivery.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing** standard, which provides a comprehensive **compliance framework** and **interoperability standards** for manufacturing-focused DTs. Key aspects include:

1. **Relevant Industry Standards**:
   - **ISO 23247 (2021)**: The foundational standard for DTs in manufacturing, structured into four parts:
     - **Part 1**: Overview and general principles, defining the DT’s purpose and scope.
     - **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)**: The primary reference, which mandates an **entity-based architecture** with four core entities (**Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, **User Entity**, and **Functional Entities (FEs)**) and their interactions.
     - **Part 3**: Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, specifying how OMEs (e.g., equipment, processes) are modeled.
     - **Part 4**: Information exchange protocols, emphasizing standardized data formats and communication methods.
   - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: Explicitly suggested in the standard as a protocol for interoperability between entities. OPC UA ensures secure, platform-independent data exchange for tasks such as device control, data collection, and user interface integration.

2. **Specifications Followed**:
   - **Entity-Based Design**: The DT must implement the **four core entities** and their **Functional Entities (FEs)**, with each entity responsible for distinct functionalities (e.g., data acquisition, modeling, user interaction).
   - **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) Representation**: The **Digital Twin Entity** must accurately reflect the OME’s observable attributes (e.g., operational states, performance metrics) using data sourced from the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**.
   - **Modularity and Interoperability**: The standard mandates that entities communicate via standardized interfaces (e.g., OPC UA), enabling seamless integration with external systems (e.g., enterprise software, IoT platforms).

3. **Certification Requirements**:
   - The standard does not explicitly outline certification processes but implies that compliance with **ISO 23247** and **OPC UA** may serve as de facto certification criteria for DT systems in manufacturing. Organizations may pursue additional certifications (e.g., **IEC 62424** for industrial data models) to further validate interoperability and data integrity.

4. **Compliance Frameworks**:
   - **ISO 23247 Compliance**: DT systems must align with the standard’s **reference architecture**, ensuring modularity, real-time synchronization, and user-centric design.
   - **OPC UA Conformance**: Entities must adhere to OPC UA’s information models and service specifications to guarantee interoperability across vendors and platforms.
   - **Data Governance**: While not explicitly detailed, the standard implies adherence to **data quality principles** (e.g., accuracy, consistency) to ensure the **Digital Twin Entity**’s reliability.

5. **Interoperability Standards Adopted**:
   - **OPC UA**: The primary standard for data exchange between entities, enabling:
     - **Device Control**: The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** uses OPC UA to interface with physical sensors/actuators.
     - **User Interaction**: The **User Entity** leverages OPC UA to query the **Digital Twin Entity** or issue commands to the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**.
     - **Functional Integration**: FEs communicate via OPC UA to share processed data or analytics results.
   - **Industry-Specific Standards**: Depending on the OME (e.g., automotive, aerospace), additional standards (e.g., **ASAM OpenMCDA** for vehicle diagnostics) may be integrated to enhance interoperability with domain-specific tools.

The standardization framework ensures that the DT system is **modular, interoperable, and compliant** with manufacturing-specific requirements, facilitating seamless integration with existing industrial ecosystems.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The security and safety considerations for the Digital Twin system, as referenced in the provided documents, are primarily governed by **ISO 26262-1:2018 (Road vehicles – Functional safety)** and **ISO 15622:2018 (Intelligent Transport Systems – Adaptive Cruise Control Systems)**. Key technical measures include:

1. **Functional Safety Management (ISO 26262-1:2018, Clause 4-8)**:
   - Implementation of a **safety lifecycle** (ASIL decomposition, risk assessment, and mitigation) aligned with **Automotive Safety Integrity Levels (ASIL)** to ensure deterministic behavior in safety-critical components.
   - **Safety requirements** derived from hazard analysis (e.g., FMEA, FTA) with traceability to system-level safety goals (e.g., **Safety Goal (SG)** decomposition into **Safety Requirements (SR)**).
   - **Safety mechanisms** such as **watchdog timers**, **redundancy**, and **fail-safe states** to prevent catastrophic failures in adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems (ISO 15622:2018, Clause 5.4).

2. **Cybersecurity Protocols (ISO 26262-1:2018, Clause 8.4.4)**:
   - **Secure communication channels** (e.g., **CAN FD**, **Ethernet AVB**) with **message authentication codes (MACs)** and **cryptographic integrity checks** to prevent tampering in V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services (3GPP TS 22.185, Release 15, Section 5.2.2).
   - **Access control** via **role-based authorization** (e.g., **OAuth 2.0** for cloud-based Digital Twin interfaces) and **hardware-based security modules (HSMs)** for cryptographic key management (implied in ISO 26262-1:2018 for ASIL-B/C systems).

3. **Threat Mitigation Strategies**:
   - **Denial-of-Service (DoS) protection** via **rate limiting**, **packet filtering**, and **priority-based scheduling** (ISO 15622:2018, Clause 6.3.2).
   - **Tamper-proofing** of Digital Twin models using **immutable ledgers** (e.g., blockchain-based audit trails for model versioning, as suggested in model-driven architectures for interactive Digital Twin cockpits [Dalibor et al., 2020, p. 382]).

4. **Fail-Safe Mechanisms (ISO 15622:2018, Clause 5.5)**:
   - **Graceful degradation** in ACC systems (e.g., transition to manual control if sensor data integrity is compromised) with **timeouts** for critical operations (e.g., **<100ms** response time for emergency braking).
   - **Redundant sensors** (e.g., LiDAR + radar) with **cross-validation** to detect and mitigate sensor failures (ISO 26262-1:2018, Clause 7.4.5).

5. **Risk Assessment Approaches**:
   - **Quantitative risk analysis (QRA)** using **probabilistic methods** (e.g., **Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)**) to assign **ASIL levels** to Digital Twin components (ISO 26262-1:2018, Annex A).
   - **Safety validation** via **hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing** and **model-in-the-loop (MIL)** simulations (Dalibor et al., 2020, p. 379) to verify fail-safe behaviors under adversarial conditions.

6. **Safety-Critical System Considerations**:
   - **Deterministic real-time execution** for Digital Twin updates (e.g., **<1ms latency** for critical path computations, as implied by ISO 15622:2018 for ACC systems).
   - **Safety-critical data isolation** via **memory protection units (MPUs)** and **sandboxed execution environments** for Digital Twin simulations (ISO 26262-1:2018, Clause 8.4.3).
   - **Compliance with ISO 26262-6 (Software)** for **static/dynamic analysis tools** (e.g., **Coverity**, **Polyspace**) to detect vulnerabilities in Digital Twin software modules.
  "
]
